# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import express, { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');

interface DecodedToken {
  userId: string;
}

export const authenticateMiddleware = async (
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction
) => {
  try {
    const tokenString = req.header('Authorization').replace('Bearer ', '');
    let decodedToken: DecodedToken | any;

    if (tokenString === null) {
      return res.status(401).send({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
    }

    decodedToken = jwt.decode(tokenString, { complete: true }) as any;
    const userId = decodedToken.payload.userId;
    req.user = { userId };

    next();
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(500).send({ error: 'Authentication failed' });
  }
};
